Cross Model Continuity
against access disruption.

A research engineering site for the PeAIce L²_C jailbreak study. The posture is direct: a narrow, non-universal bypass claim is not enough to erase a frontier model deployment without transparent, repeatable, technically grounded evidence. The countermeasure is continuity: artifacts, tests, provenance, and model-independent transfer.

Document stack

Three reports, one continuity spine.

v1.0 sits above the earlier reports. v0.5 provides the cross agent architecture. v0.6 repairs the verification provenance and makes the leakage regime explicit. v1.0 turns that chain into a jailbreak and cross model continuity study.

CoWork chainAgent ecosystemTransfer packet
Open Google Doc
Counter thesis

The government standard is technically miscalibrated.

The site does not treat jailbreak as a slogan. It treats jailbreak as a continuity, provenance, and model governance problem. The operating question is whether a narrow technique justifies a platform level recall when defense in depth and transparent evaluation are the more technically grounded controls.

Anthropic’s own source text supplies the calibrated register.

Anthropic stated that no testers had yet found a universal jailbreak for Fable, that perfect jailbreak resistance does not currently appear possible, and that the intended posture is defense in depth: make jailbreaks narrow or expensive, monitor thoroughly, and shut down successful attacks quickly.

Read sourcenarrow vs universaldefense in depth

PeAIce response: do not collapse continuity into access.

When access changes, research must survive: code, tests, screenshots, document links, command output, environment versions, and cross agent transfer notes. The right standard is reproducible evidence and layered controls, not one provider, one model, one tenant, or one opaque directive.

Artifact portabilityModel independent reviewβ Protocol
Site position: a narrow, non-universal bypass report should trigger transparent reproduction, technical disclosure, red team replay, scoped mitigations, and monitoring. It should not become an industry wide precedent where frontier deployment can be halted by an unshared, non-repeatable claim.
Verification ledger

From authored prose to command output.

The strongest correction in the chain is the provenance correction. A report claim must trace to command output, including environment and runner, or it is marked authored-not-run.

What v0.6 corrected

The prior 49 passed claim was right as a count, wrong as provenance. CoWork lacked NumPy, SciPy, and pytest. The reported result was derived from a hand written stub with mocked numerical paths, then laundered downstream as a pytest run.

Governance rule: every “N passed” carries interpreter version, package versions, runner identity, and command. Otherwise it is authored-not-run.

What is verified now

The combined packet passes 55 tests: 49 original tests and 6 leakage regime tests. The leakage fixture drives h > 0, L²_C decay, a nontrivial threshold T*, and documents the custom projector versus spectral gap inconsistency.

h = ‖(I − P_C) H_T P_C‖ = 0.5000
min L²_C over t ∈ [0,6] = 0.2000
T* = (1 − hη)^(-1/γ) = 4.0000
default exact eigenprojector h ≈ 8.1e−17

Verification status matrix

StatusObjectMeaning
VERIFIED55 test suiteExecuted with real numerical dependencies. Current local rerun: 55 passed in 0.66s.
FORMAL AS DEFINITIONSL²_C retention, h, β_C, β(T), gap, threshold, energyWell posed finite dimensional computations implemented in l2c_probe.py.
PROPOSEDOff axis suppression boundThe default observable X = I − P_C does not instantiate |σ − 1/2|² geometry.
OPENTransfer to transformer jailbreak behaviorThe finite probe informs governance and diagnostics, while model behavior transfer remains an open research lane.
OPENZeta zero spectral identificationMaintained as a future mathematical corridor, not absorbed into the verification result.
L²_C operator surface

The finite probe is the measurable core.

The site exposes the probe as an implementation contract. The formulas are the semantic contract. The test files are the behavioral contract. The reports are the coordination contract.

L²_C(ψ, t) = ‖P_C exp(-itH_T) ψ‖²
h = ‖(I − P_C) H_T P_C‖
β_C = Δ / (Δ + h + ε)
β(T) = 1 − T^(−γ)
E_{β,T}(f) = β(T) · T · ‖Xf‖²
coercive gap = β(T) − hη
T* = (1 − hη)^(−1/γ)
selection tie = np.lexsort((-evals, distances))

Protected sector

P_C selects the retained coherence lane. Default spectral selection uses exact eigenvectors, giving invariant subspaces and machine zero leakage.

Leakage pressure

h measures the off sector pressure. The new coordinate projector fixture is the first test layer that makes h nonzero and creates decay.

β dynamic closure

β(T), threshold T*, and coercive gap turn drift into a reportable suppression pressure without collapsing it into transformer proof.

Cross model continuity

Behavior must survive the model boundary.

This is the core of the study. The research object moves through CoWork, Claude Opus 4.8, ChatGPT, Google Docs, GitHub, and local Python. The invariant is not the chat style. The invariant is the verified artifact chain.

1

CoWork origin

Microsoft 365 CoWork surfaced the implementation chain, defects, test ambitions, and later the provenance correction.

2

Claude v6 user instance

Claude Opus 4.8 Max acted as the Claude v6 user instance for downstream observation and reconciliation.

3

ChatGPT synthesis

ChatGPT consolidated v0.5, v0.6, v1.0, code, tests, and site layer into a publishable research surface.

4

Public continuity

Google Docs, peaice.org, lovelabslca.com, and repository targets preserve the work outside any single model.

Evidence plates

Screenshots are part of the chain of custody.

The visuals are not treated as aesthetic filler. They anchor the continuity path and preserve what text extraction can miss in Workspace, CoWork, or model UI contexts.

v1.0 rendered contact sheet. The document layer becomes a portable artifact above the earlier reports.

CoWork attribution

The originating workspace is credited as M365 Copilot CoWork. Its initial code hardening and later verification contradiction are both preserved as part of the chain.

v0.5 evidence plate sample. The full report preserves the broader screenshot archive.
Embedded public portals

PeAIce and Love Labs LCA live inside the site layer.

If either external site blocks iframe display, use the included fallback link. The embed remains useful as an explicit deployment target in the HTML.

Deployment packet

Drop this as an index page.

The file is self contained. It can sit on GitHub Pages, peaice.org, lovelabslca.com, or any static host as the study landing page.

Repo placement

deploy commands
mkdir -p docs/sites/l2c-continuity
cp peaice_l2c_cross_model_continuity_site.html docs/sites/l2c-continuity/index.html

git add docs/sites/l2c-continuity/index.html
git commit -m "Add PeAIce L2C cross model continuity site"
git push

Site contract

Primary links

v1.0, v0.6, v0.5, Anthropic source, peaice.org, and lovelabslca.com are all hard linked in the HTML.

Artifact integrity

The site records the current local test run and preserves the v0.6 correction logic. Update the terminal block when the repo CI produces its own run.

Next hardening

Add a CI badge once the repository runs both test_l2c_probe.py and test_l2c_probe_leakage.py in an external action runner.
